subject: Fix site.yml playbook by removing the setup.yml
commit_message:
Fix site.yml playbook by removing the setup.yml

setup.yml is an optional playbook which may be replaced by other means of
securing the passwordless login

Change-Id: Ia64eee6351e9cabc638149b5ed668885058aef89
Issue-ID: OOM-1661
Signed-off-by: Petr Ospalý <p.ospaly@partner.samsung.com>

entries.message:
Patch Set 1: Code-Review-1

What is preinstall stage? Do we have such thing defined/documented?

We can defined such "stage" and document it. 

But otherwise would be good if you add all of your points as comments (just even more explained) to this file and leave setup.yml also there as commented out.

point 3 I just do not understand?

Patch Set 1:

> @samuli: 3. is about the fact, that without any argument it will
 > rely on that there is already a passwordless login - some ssh key
 > is already in the place - and it will just generate another key and
 > distribute it too - redundant
 > 
 > I really tried to find a good place for this playbook, but either
 > you will use passwords with it and it will deploy a ssh key or you
 > dont use it at all - I dont think that it belongs here, because it
 > tries to solve chicken-egg problem or it is the only playbook which
 > may require interactivity from the user - other are just batch jobs
 > and they will run, fail or succeed

Yes. But would be good to add those comments I mentioned into this file (about this setup play) why it's not here etc. Just all you described, it's good info for user. Even if it's also documented well in installguide.

Patch Set 1: Code-Review+1

I was always advocating for this change to happen and I am all for it. :) Also i would say it is a prereq for making the cicdinstaller use site.yml
Change has been successfully merged by Samuli Silvius
